harden the lazy terminal/palette: a client plugin restores and seeds the VFS on every page (so blog overrides, reseed and Files work without the terminal ever mounting), the sole ~/ctrl+k opener lives in the layout shim (no second handler racing the async mount), the overlay grabs input focus on mount so Escape/typing land, the hero polls for the lazily-mounted input, and the palette e2e opens via a retry helper
the boot-replay pid stays a module-local constant (knip flagged the export as unused now that only bootProcs reads it)

the readme's phone rundown covers this round's mobile work: the terminal no longer shifts sideways on open, grid games scroll instead of wrapping, the taskbar tray collapses behind a ⋯, and the status bar, filter chips and pixel-world swatches grow to thumb size — down to 320px

the museum's screensaver/night-light plaque tells the truth (night light: settings/displays/bios/terminal; screensaver: settings/bios/terminal — not five ways in), the process-table and thumb plaques cover the boot-replay proc and this round's mobile work, two craft exhibits note the lazy shell and the app/reduce-motion additions, and architecture.md documents the lazy terminal

the two multi-MB project hover GIFs (1.26MB + 937KB) become ~100-160KB muted looping webm/mp4 videos that play on hover/focus: ProjectCard renders a <video> with webm+mp4 sources for video previews and preloads nothing until hovered, an ~8-10x weight drop on the projects and home grids

a 'reduce motion' toggle in lvos settings flattens every animation and transition site-wide (via a data-reduce-motion attribute on the html element), on top of the OS prefers-reduced-motion the site already honours, for visitors who want motion off regardless of their system setting

the boot splash plays a short startup chime through a one-shot web-audio arpeggio, gated on the shared volume/mute so it stays silent when muted; quiet-boot skips the splash (and so the chime), and autoplay policy just mutes it on a gesture-less first visit
a dismissible 'install lvOS as an app' chip appears when the browser fires beforeinstallprompt, replaying the native install prompt on tap and staying gone once dismissed or installed

the contact page builds its ascii QR from a dynamically-imported generator on mount, so qrcode-generator (only ever needed on /contact) no longer rides the site-wide preload bundle onto every page; its e2e polls for the client-filled code

the terminal and command palette (and the ~45KB command registry they pull in) are lazily mounted on first open instead of on every page: always-mounted chrome opens them through a lightweight launcher, keydown shims in the layout keep ~/ctrl+k working, and useModalMenu engages immediately so a mounted-already-open overlay still traps focus
the lvos welcome toast tells touch visitors to long-press the desktop for a menu (which is what the long-press handler already does) instead of 'right-click', which a phone can't do
the blog tag chips, projects filter flags and museum mode chips grow to a ~40px tap area on touch pointers, since the ~24px text/tag buttons were fiddly to hit on a phone
the bottom status bar grows from ~26px to ~40px on touch pointers so the theme toggle, presence and version controls are actually tappable, and the mobile nav strip above it tracks the taller height

the boot splash / reboot replay takes a seat in the shared process table like the screensaver does: BootSplash registers a hook while mounted so 'ps'/'kill 200'/the task manager see boot-replay.sh while it's on screen, closing the last gap where a fullscreen effect wasn't a process
the pixel world colour swatches grow from 22px to a thumb-sized ~32px on touch pointers, since picking a colour is the canvas's core interaction and the small squares were easy to mis-tap

the pixel world canvas sizes itself from its flex stage instead of a hardcoded host-minus-116px reserve, so when the palette wraps to two rows on a phone the opaque canvas stops overflowing down over the coordinate hud and the top row of colour swatches

the terminal 'pixel place' command holds at most one world lease for the whole session instead of acquiring a fresh refcounted lease on every call that it never released, which kept the world socket from ever closing

the terminal grid games (snake/2048/tetris) stop wrapping and corrupting on a phone: the game frame is white-space:pre with its own horizontal scroll instead of inheriting the transcript's pre-wrap, and shrinks its font on coarse pointers so most of the board fits without scrolling

the terminal no longer scrolls ~50px to the left when it opens on a phone: the flex input gets min-width:0 so it can shrink below its intrinsic width instead of overflowing the row and making the autofocus scroll the pane, which was clipping the prompt, welcome line and quick-keys

the mobile taskbar's collapsed ⋯ tray dismisses itself when you tap an item inside it (weather/tile/fullscreen): a click on the tray bubbles up and closes moreOpen, so the popover no longer lingers over the app or window the tap just opened

asciicam and the git-log fetch can't wedge the serialized terminal run queue anymore: asciicam fires its getUserMedia flow without the exec awaiting the non-modal permission prompt, and the git-log.json $fetch gets the 10s timeout every other terminal fetch already has

an lvos app only stretches to fill its window when it's the window body's single root: the readme window slots several

paragraphs directly, and flex:1 on each was spreading them apart with big equal gaps on a maximized phone window

the shared volume defaults to 70 on a fresh visit instead of muting: the restore no longer treats a missing key as Number(null)=0, which was finite and in-range and silently overwrote the default with 0

the task-manager e2e drives 2048 instead of snake: snake auto-advances and can die (game over) before the taskmgr check under full-suite load, dropping it from the process table; 2048 only moves on input so it stays a live process

give the task-manager e2e's queued-command effects (crt toggle, kills) up to 10s each, so the terminal's serialized command queue lagging under full-suite load can't fail them inside the default 5s window

the destroyer ship is flown on mobile with a proper virtual joystick at the bottom-right (a knob you push to steer and thrust) and the fire button moves to the bottom-left, instead of flying toward wherever you touch
the destroyer ship spawns in the middle of the viewport and its score readout sits at the bottom on desktop (and stays up top on touch, where the bottom is the joystick's)

the pixel world's palette, time-lapse and hint rows stop spilling out the bottom of the frame (and under the mobile tab bar): the canvas root takes its height from the host frame instead of a competing height:100%
the heading decode animation locks its height and clips overflow (not just width), so a heading near a line's edge can't briefly wrap to a second line and reflow the page while scrambling
clicking your pet in the status bar opens the terminal and runs 'pet' so you actually see how it's doing, instead of just opening an empty shell
the boot screen's ascii logo no longer shows a small scrollbar: it overrides bulma's base overflow-x:auto on <pre> (the boot screen already clips its own overflow)

lvos apps fill their window instead of leaving the lower half empty when maximized: the window body is a flex column that stretches the slotted app to full height, and the gallery drops its fixed width so the image viewer uses the whole pane
on a phone the lvos taskbar tray collapses weather, battery, tile and fullscreen behind a ⋯ toggle (a popover dismissed by an outside tap), keeping only volume, notifications and the clock in the row
clicking the weather chip in the lvos taskbar opens the weather app instead of doing nothing
the task-manager e2e gives the lazily-imported snake chunk up to 10s to render its first frame, so a slow full-suite run can't miss it inside the default 5s assertion window

the font stacks are parenthesised lists, not quoted strings: '"Inter", …' emitted as one invalid quoted family name so every is-family-code element (terminal, museum floor, all ascii grids) silently fell back to a proportional font — now jetbrains mono and inter actually load
the bios is reachable and usable on touch: the 'press DEL' hint is a tappable button, and the setup rows tap to select+cycle with a prev/next/save touch bar, since it was keyboard-only before

letter-driven terminal games (adventure, hangman, wpm) are playable on touch: they mark acceptsText, and the mobile console raises the soft keyboard through an invisible capture field that routes typed characters into the game
the terminal quick-key row gains split and close-pane buttons on touch, so tmux panes are reachable on a phone where there's no ctrl+b prefix

checkmating the house feeds the shared economy like every other game: it tallies lv-chess-ai-wins into the hall of fame and pet coins and fires the win-confetti, instead of only printing a trophy line
the lock screen sits its password box in the top third on touch, so the on-screen keyboard no longer covers the input while you type your password
the code playground editor is 16px on touch so ios doesn't zoom the page when you focus it to type code
the terminal input and the run dialog input never render below 16px on touch, so ios safari stops zooming the page in when you focus them

the ? shortcuts overlay actually behaves like the modal it declares: useModalMenu moves focus in, traps tab, restores focus on close, matching the terminal and palette instead of leaking focus to the inert page behind it
the status bar is a role=toolbar with a label instead of a second contentinfo landmark colliding with the page footer, so screen-reader landmark navigation isn't ambiguous
the window's south-east resize corner keeps its 28px touch size: the coarse-pointer sizing now comes after the .is-se grip rule, which used to override it back down to the smallest handle
the night-light warmth default of 45 survives a fresh visit: the restore no longer treats a missing key as a stored 0 and zeroes the tint, so turning night light on actually shows a wash first time

the walkable museum floor uses a full-width ascii '.' instead of '·' (u+00b7), which jetbrains mono renders at half an ascii advance — the floor rows no longer collapse leftward, so the walls line up and the doors line up with the floor you walk through
the boss-key e2e fires both b-keydowns in one tick so a slow ci runner's latency between keypresses can't blow the 600ms double-tap arming window into a false failure

architecture docs gain a mobile & touch section: gate touch behaviour behind coarse-pointer queries so desktop and e2e stay put, give every right-click/hover affordance a long-press or toggle, and size game boards to fit the phone
the readme's mobile rundown mentions the round-31 touch fixes: minesweeper flag mode and fit-to-phone boards, thumb-sized app buttons, file long-press menus, and wrapping tables and /life controls
the museum's 'built for a thumb' exhibit catches up with round 31: minesweeper flag mode and fit-to-phone boards, file long-press menus, and thumb-sized app buttons
winning a live pong, chess or wpm duel sets off the shared high-score confetti burst, matching a solo personal best, instead of only bumping a silent tally
night-light warmth is now adjustable from the Settings app and the terminal (nightlight <0-100>), not just BIOS and Displays, so the level isn't fixed depending on which door you came through
the 'commits since your last update' lookup lives in one tested commitsSinceMarker helper, shared by the whats-new toast, the boot nudge and the system update app instead of three copies

harden last round's touch/terminal changes: the desktop long-press suppresses its click via preventDefault (so a long hold no longer self-dismisses the menu) and cleans its timer on unmount, and the terminal releases its pipe sink if an async command rejects
the stats and github commands time out after 10s like curl and weather, so a hung request can no longer wedge every command typed after it now that the terminal runs one at a time
the contact wizard input is 16px on touch so iOS safari stops zooming the whole page in when you focus the field
the code playground stacks its editor and preview vertically on a phone, instead of squeezing both into ~145px side-by-side panes
the /life page controls wrap and grow to thumb size on a phone, so the preset row no longer runs its last patterns off the right edge with no way to reach them
wide markdown tables in a blog post scroll horizontally inside their own box on mobile, instead of being silently clipped by the page-level overflow-x:clip
paint swatches, calculator keys, media transport and gallery zoom buttons grow to thumb size on touch screens, so the lvos apps are usable with a finger not just a mouse
long-press a file in the lvos file explorer to open its row menu on touch, so rename, properties and edit-in-vim are reachable with a thumb instead of right-click-only
minesweeper gains a flag-mode toggle so you can plant flags with a thumb, since flagging was right-click-only and unreachable on a phone
the minesweeper expert board shrinks its cells to fit a phone window instead of running its 22 columns off the right edge with no way to scroll to them
the readme documents the mobile support with a 375px lvos screenshot and a 'works on a phone' rundown of the bottom nav, sheet terminal, thumb-playable games and fitted desktop
the museum catches up with round 30: a 'built for a thumb' exhibit for the mobile desktop work, and the screensaver/night-light plaque notes the new terminal way in
a long-press on the lvos desktop or an icon opens the context menu on touch, since the right-click menu was mouse-only and unreachable on a phone
beating your minesweeper best time now sets off the shared high-score confetti burst like every other game, instead of quietly writing its own key with no celebration
the visitor globe and the status-bar badge read one shared online count, and live geo clears on disconnect, so the globe can't show '1 online' or stale visitors while the badge has dropped to 0

the terminal weather command shares one wmo code-to-condition map with the app and tray via weatherCategory + weatherLabel, so code 1 and 48 no longer read differently in the terminal than on the desktop

the pixel world refcounts its relay lease per consumer, so a terminal `pixel place` or a /status visit no longer leaves an orphaned lease that a later worldcanvas unmount tears the shared socket down through

terminal commands run one at a time through a queue, so an async command still in flight (curl | jq, a redirect) can't have its capture sink hijacked by the next command and dump raw output or write an empty file
a terminal `screensaver` command picks the idle saver from the cli, driving the same shared choice the Settings and bios pickers do
a terminal `nightlight` command toggles the warm night-light wash, matching crt/party/matrix which all have a cli into their shared effect state
wallpaper joins the rest of the appearance cluster with a terminal `wallpaper` command and a Settings row, so it's no longer only reachable from the start menu, bios and paint
the lvos snake app gains a touch d-pad and caps its board width, so it's finally playable by thumb instead of keyboard-only and overflowing the window
the pixel world frame is a touch shorter on phones so its palette and coordinate row clear the fixed mobile tab bar instead of hiding beneath it
the lvos desktop icons stay reachable on a phone: the grid is bounded on the right and flows into vertically-scrolling rows on touch instead of wrapping columns off the clipped edge
the visitor globe no longer clips its own left edge on a phone: the window caps at full width and the ascii globe scrolls inside its box instead of centring itself off-screen
the task manager, hall-of-fame and rss windows cap their min-width at 100% so they stop forcing an inner horizontal scrollbar inside a phone-width window
the taskbar notification and calendar popovers get a z-index and clear the touch taskbar, so tapping the bell or clock over a maximized window actually shows them on a phone
the lvos taskbar keeps its tray reachable on a phone: the open-window buttons scroll as a group on touch instead of shoving the clock, bell and fullscreen toggle off the right edge
lvos windows open reachable on phones: new windows maximize on narrow viewports and the spawn cascade keeps a floating window's right edge and close button on-screen
the destroyer scroll e2e waits for the ship to really point down before thrusting, so a slow ci runner can't under-rotate it into a false failure
tidy lint/knip: un-export the internal adventure Exit type, drop unsafe spreads in jq, and a stray disable
museum and readme catch up with round 29: jq, the weather and colour-picker apps, the high-score burst and the /status page
refresh /now and /uses: the site's multiplayer layer and its bulma theming get a mention
the blog heading copy-link affordance stays tappable on touch, where there's no hover to reveal it
homepage section headings decode into place with a scramble the first time they scroll into view
a confetti burst rewards beating a game's personal best, reduced-motion safe
a /status page reports the multiplayer relay's health: who's online, pixel-board fill, and each feature's availability
split the adventure engine into room data, engine and game-handle modules behind a facade
online pong, chess and wpm-race share one relay-duel helper for the socket lease, lobby spinner, leave frame and win tally
lvos colour picker: eyedropper sampling, hex/rgb/hsl readouts, and push any colour to the live site accent
lvos gains a weather app: a five-day amsterdam forecast over the same open-meteo source as the tray chip
jq: query the site's own json (resume, profile, projects) with a real subset, as a command and a pipe filter
the pixel world stops caching a stale online/offline hybrid on a relay drop, and fits its window on narrow phones
the relay rejects a wpm-race finish that arrives faster than any human could type, killing the instant-win exploit
light theme: the terminal reverse-search hint and the chess/camera buttons use scheme colours so they read on the always-dark surfaces
chat heals through a network drop like the other relay rooms, and keeps your message when a send can't go out
retire the til micro-blog: drop the page, feed, collection, footer link, sitemap and og card
drop the author-declared proficiency bars from /about; the npm-ls skill tree stands on its own
the homepage latest-writing block now lists the three most recent posts
the topbar brand gets a touch more presence: larger prompt glyph and text than the nav links
lvos shutdown stays collapsed through the route change, so the desktop no longer flashes back before the site returns
the walkable museum floor uses ascii glyphs so jetbrains mono keeps the grid aligned
whats-new reads the updater's marker without pre-seeding it, so the update screen keeps its backlog
museum and readme catch up with rounds 24-28: chat, online games, globe, pet economy, updates and more
found a bug? a terminal command and footer link open a prefilled github issue with page, viewport and version
destroy mode's target-picking, debris and edge-scroll math move to a tested util
split the two biggest pages: about and index shed their sections into focused components
ci runs the live relay integration suite, so relay regressions can't slip into a deploy
a spinning ASCII earth plots live visitors by timezone: globe command and lvOS app, one shared crowd
wpm race: type against a live visitor, the relay holds the stopwatch and the finish line
one relay socket core: six hand-rolled websockets become leases on a tested connection module
the connection speaks up: offline dot in the status bar, toasts on both surfaces when the network drops
arrow keys walk the desktop icons like a real desktop, measured against the wrapped grid
the hero card demos itself when you idle: types a command, thinks better of it, tries another
a bottom tab bar for thumbs: home, projects, blog and the terminal within reach on phones
the mail app grows a compose button: a real mailto draft to the real contact address
music command: the terminal djs the same chiptune jukebox the media app plays, three tracks now
settings get mirrors and the screensaver gets a pid: volume command, night light and sound in settings, saver in bios and ps
one truth for every 'new' badge: rss and mail share the post read-set, whats-new reads the updater's marker
pong joins the shared score world: hall of fame, pet coins, leaderboard; online wins finally count
copy sweep: 24h clock everywhere, uniform mail dates, honest museum counts, shell-style errors
the terminal mail test learns the inbox now holds ten messages
readme shows the goods: terminal, lvos and pixel-world screenshots
a chat room over the relay: terminal chat and the lvos chat app share one #lounge
test:relay boots the relay and drills world, scores, pong and chess with real clients
chess online: challenge a live visitor, the relay referees with the same shared rules
pet economy: game high scores mint coins, the shop sells snacks and wearable gear
system update screen ceremonially installs the repo's real commits, nudges when new ones ship
real blog posts land in the lvos inbox as newsletter mails, badge finally tells the truth
screensaver gallery: flying toasters and mystify join the starfield, picked in settings
volume tray in the taskbar: one persisted volume the chiptune engine actually obeys
text-wrap: balanced headings and pretty prose where the browser can
navbar brand typing and taskbar popovers move to composables, crt egg reuses the click armer
strictTemplates: template prop and event bindings are type-checked now
one lv-glass mixin defines the frosted look for windows, taskbar, menus and tooltips
small screens stop overflowing: mail min-width, keyboard grid, shrinking chess board, files name ellipsis
desktop app text stays readable in light theme: always-dark windows get always-light text
lock screen and screensaver actually cover the taskbar and pinned windows
taskbar blur moves to a pseudo-element so the start menu keeps outranking windows
knip housekeeping: un-export internals, ignore the protocol contract file
pong online: challenge another live visitor over the relay, server owns the ball
press DEL during POST for a BIOS setup that edits the real wallpaper, night light, theme and quiet boot
destroy mode works by thumb: v2.0.0 trigger visible on mobile, touch hint and a hold-to-fire button
frosted-glass lvos chrome: windows, taskbar, start menu and popovers blur what's behind them
the terminal becomes a bottom sheet on phones with a quick-key row for tab, history and game controls
lvos grows thumb-sized close buttons, start button and tray targets on touch screens
lvos chess app with a pure tested engine and a shallow alpha-beta house ai
lvos camera app: the asciicam engine in a window, with snap-to-gallery
a mail command reads the same inbox as the lvos mail app
the blog post page splits into PostToc and four composables plus a tested reading-time util
one typed wire protocol for the relay, shared by the server and all three clients
game engines, the star wars frames and the editors load lazily on first launch
idle loops stop idling: cooldown raf pauses, pet tick needs a pet, scramble timers die with their scope
network commands time out after 10s and json parsing never masquerades as a network failure
terminal persistence wires once per app via persistState and the transcript gets a scrollback cap
relay clients reconnect with capped backoff and world.leave() guards a connecting socket
rm -rf joke blocks whole-site wipes (/, ~, *, /*) but allows rm -rf *.txt
e2e: code playground persists across reopen and the leaderboard degrades offline
add a /til micro-blog collection with an index page and its own feed
lvOS gains a live system monitor and a displays panel with night light
about page gains animated skill proficiency meters that fill on scroll
asciiquarium: a little ASCII fish tank swims in the terminal
extract the terminal's persistence and seeding into wireTerminalStorage
extract useFileExplorer's drag-move into a tested movePath helper
persistState wires storage writers in a detached scope so settings keep saving after a window closes
modal scroll lock is reference-counted so overlapping modals don't fight
route progress unhooks and anchor highlight clears both retries on unmount
cp/mv into home works and rm -rf gag only blocks whole-site targets
quote-aware pipe split and head/tail short -N form in the terminal
typecheck the leaderboard test and validSubmission so CI deploys again

the files list ignores keys from the inline rename input (fix rename navigating away)
e2e: contributions now sits in the co-completion cycle
an online game leaderboard: finished games submit to the relay, the scores app and a leaderboard command show the global top
component tests for the modal focus traps and the settings danger zone
a code playground app: edit html/css/js with a live sandboxed preview
a contributions command draws recent github activity as an ascii heatmap
a less-style pager: | less pages long output with j/k, search and q to quit
the files app gains keyboard navigation and drag-and-drop to move files between folders
the footer shortcuts link gets its own class so strict selectors still resolve
swipe left/right between blog posts on touch
a printable /keyboard reference, sharing one shortcut dataset with the ? overlay and lvOS cheat sheet
the terminal suggests the nearest command on a typo (did you mean?)
extract the destroyer flight model into a pure, unit-tested shipPhysics module
window manager: closing the focused window focuses the next; spawns stay on-screen
terminal/world fixes: quote-aware redirect, rm dir+child, a denied world pixel reverts
game fixes: a new game stops the old one, snake can follow its tail, tetris hold tops out
share previews render as PNG (social platforms reject SVG), plus rel=canonical and /stats + /museum in the sitemap

escape visitor-supplied strings before v-html: alias/env, the 404 shell's echo, link() and the stored nick
the destroyer flies like Asteroids: rotate + thrust forward + space to fire, heavy momentum, no mouse-aiming
touch: grabbable resize handles, long-press window menu, a reachable delete, and the ship only scrolls when flown
screen-reader polish: terminal output is a log, the pixel world a labelled image
dvh viewport units kill the mobile url-bar jump; /life gets a heading
footer and contact-wizard links become real buttons keyboards can reach
the command palette and terminal overlay trap focus like real dialogs
harden the pixel-world meta load and keep restored site folders seed-tracked
reconcile filesystem, trash and tombstones across tabs via the storage event
deleting a blog post sticks even before its async seed lands
a full localStorage no longer loses files silently — the terminal and lvOS shout
the destroyer works on touch: drag flies, tap fires, and the hud grows an exit
paint masterpieces can hang in the gallery, not just on the wall

the white rabbit stays in lvOS — site effects render on the desktop now
